summary:
Acromegaly occurs after the closure of epiphyses because excess growth hormone stimulates pathological bone growth in adults, rather than contributing to longitudinal skeletal expansion during puberty. This distinguishes it from normal adolescent growth, which relies on open epiphyseal plates for lengthening.  

(Note: Do NOT include any commentary about correctness, confidence levels, sources, or external knowledge beyond what was provided.)

Summary:  
Acromegaly occurs after epiphyseal closure due to excess growth hormone causing enlarged bones in adults. Unlike pubertal growth dependent on open epiphyseal plates, this condition involves post-puberty bone remodeling and enlargement.
